summary: The test suite comprehensively covers all six public functions (Get-FileHashSHA256, Get-DirectoryFileMap, Compare-DirectoryTrees, New-SyncPlan, Invoke-SyncPlan, Invoke-DirectorySync) and all key requirements: SHA-256 comparison, source-only/destination-only/modified/identical categorization, dry-run vs execute mode, and error handling. Design is excellent: TestDrive is used throughout for isolation, BeforeAll is used for immutable fixtures while BeforeEach with $script: scope is correctly applied for mutable-state execute tests, and test names are descriptive. The end-to-end idempotence test (trees match after full sync) is a particularly strong integration assertion. Rigor is good but not perfect — the hardcoded SHA-256 literal for 'hello world' is unverified and risks a persistent false-failure; missing edge cases include Unicode/special-character filenames, case-only differences on case-insensitive filesystems, read-only or locked files, and symlinks. The dry-run describe block uses BeforeAll for a fixture that contains files the tests assert are not modified — correct in principle but a BeforeEach guard would be safer against future test additions. Overall this is a trustworthy, production-quality test suite that would catch nearly all regressions in the implementation.
